title: Submitting to Stores and OTA Updates
description: Submit your app to the App Store and Google Play, and set up over‑the‑air updates.

slides.title: EAS Submit 🚀
Preparing Store Listings
slides.content:
## What You Need

- **App Store (iOS)**: App name, description, keywords, support URL, privacy policy URL, screenshots (various sizes), app preview video (optional).
- **Google Play (Android)**: App name, short description, full description, screenshots, feature graphic, privacy policy.

These are created on the respective store consoles, not in code.

slides.title: Over‑the‑Air (OTA) Updates with EAS Update
slides.content:
## Updating Without Rebuilding

EAS Update lets you push JavaScript and asset updates to users without going through the app store review process (as long as you don't change native code).

- Configure `expo-updates` in your project.
- Set `runtimeVersion` in `app.json` to manage compatibility.
- Publish updates with `eas update`.

This is great for quick bug fixes and content updates.

quizData.question: What does EAS Submit do?

options.text: Uploads the app to app stores

quizData.explanation: EAS Submit handles uploading your binary to App Store Connect and Google Play Console.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Use EAS Update for frequent updates, but remember that any change requiring native code (new libraries, permissions) still needs a full store submission.
